title: Calamagrostis nematophylla

The genus Calamagrostis is part of the subfamily Pooideae, which is a group of cool-season grasses. This subfamily is within the family Poaceae, also known as the true grasses. The classification of Calamagrostis nematophylla reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the grass family.

intro: Calamagrostis nematophylla, also known as the feather reed grass, is a perennial grass species native to China. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various ailments such as arthritis and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Calamagrostis nematophylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
